title: Employment and Support Allowance changes 2012 for claimants in Great Britain
body: "The government has limited the period for which Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) can be paid in some circumstances. On 1 May 2012 the Welfare Reform Act introduced two changes to contribution-based ESA:   limiting the period for which people in the Work Related Activity Group (WRAG) can receive contribution-based ESA to 365 days preventing any new claims for ESA on the grounds of youth   The rule about linking ESA claims within 104 weeks has also been abolished by separate regulations.  This document explains more about these changes. "
